questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: An optically active hydrocarbon A has molecular formula $\mathrm{C}_8 \mathrm{H}_{88}$. A on monochlorination gives five alkyl halide B to F with their molecular formula $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{Cl}$. B does not undergo dehydrohalogenation on treatment with alcoholic solution of KOH . Treatment of either C or D with alcoholic KOH yields same alkene $\mathrm{G}\left(\mathrm{C}_8 \mathrm{H}_{16}\right)$, which on ozonolysis followed by work-up with Zn -dimethyl sulphide gives an optically inactive compound $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}$ and ethanal. Also C is enantiomeric, whereas D is distereomeric. E on dehydrohalogenation yields an alkene, which on reductive ozonolysis yields $\mathrm{H}\left(\mathrm{C}_7 \mathrm{H}_{44} \mathrm{O}\right)$, which is optically inactive. H on treatment with $\mathrm{LiAlH}_4$ yields $\mathrm{I}\left(\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_{16} \mathrm{O}\right)$ which can be resolved into enantiomers. F on dehydrohalogenation yields an alkene $\left(\mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{H}_{16}\right)$, which on reductive ozonolysis yields $\mathrm{J}\left(\mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{H}_{44} \mathrm{O}\right)$, which is optically active and has same configuration as that of A. Identify A to J explaining the reactions involved.Question: Identify structure of "A" compound
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
